intro: Leucas pechuelii, commonly known as 'Pechuel's licorice', is a shrub-like plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucas pechuelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Leucas pechuelii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate colds, coughs, and other respiratory problems.
